title: The `->` operator as it appears in a Precept file — monospace font, syntax-highlighted, terminal dark background. In Fira Code or Cascadia Code, `->` renders as a ligature arrow. This is the governing transition operator: every valid path in a definition is a `->`. The icon is literally the code. It speaks directly to the developer audience in their own visual language.
conceptualBridge: The `->` code token means Precept's transitions are written as this exact operator because every valid path in a definition is declared with `->` — the icon is the language's governing symbol rendered in its native context.
decision: Shortlisted: the only candidate where the icon IS the language — `->` is completely specific to Precept's governing operator, the terminal aesthetic is new to the set, and two bold monospace chars remain legible at 32px. Fira Code ligature renders it as a single arrow glyph.
